    Classes in de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext that implement Expression
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    DLEmbeddedExpression
    This class is used to parse function applications with JavaDL escapes within set statements or similar situations.
    class 
    EscapeExpression
    Handles JML expressions that begin with an escape character '\'.

    ExpressionSVWrapper
     
    class 
    KeYAnnotationUseSpecification
     
    class 
    NewArrayWrapper
     
    class 
    NewWrapper
     
    class 
    PassiveExpression
     
    class 
    RegisteredEscapeExpression
    This class handles all escape expressions in set-statements, that are registered in JMLTranslator.jml2jdl
    class 
    RKeYMetaConstructExpression
     
    class 
    SpecialReferenceWrapper

  • Uses of Expression in recoder.java.expression

    Subinterfaces of Expression in recoder.java.expression
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    interface 
    ExpressionStatement
    An ExpressionStatement is a statement that may appear as an expression.
    Classes in recoder.java.expression that implement Expression
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    ArrayInitializer
    An ArrayInitializer is a valid expression exclusively for initializing ArrayTypes.
    class 
    Assignment
    An assignment is an operator with side-effects.
    class 
    ElementValueArrayInitializer
     
    class 
    Literal
    Literal.
    class 
    Operator
    Operator base class.
    class 
    ParenthesizedExpression
    Redundant Parentheses.

  • Uses of Expression in recoder.kit.transformation

    Constructors in recoder.kit.transformation with parameters of type Expression
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    PrependExpressionWithStatements(CrossReferenceServiceConfiguration sc, Expression x, Statement statement)
    Creates a new transformation object that inserts the given statement such that no state changes take place between the statement and the evaluation of the expression.
     
    PrependExpressionWithStatements(CrossReferenceServiceConfiguration sc, Expression x, ASTList<Statement> statements)
    Creates a new transformation object that inserts the given statements such that no state changes take place between the statements and the evaluation of the expression.
     
    ShiftPreceedingStatementExpressions(CrossReferenceServiceConfiguration sc, Expression x)
    Creates a new transformation object that shifts statement expressions to initializers of new local variables.

    Modifier and Type

    Description
    Expression
    TypingException.getUntypableExpression()
    Returns the expression that could not be typed.
    Methods in recoder.service with parameters of type Expression
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Type
    ConstantEvaluator.getCompileTimeConstantType(Expression expr)
    Returns the type of a constant expression if it is a compile-time constant as defined in the Java language specification, or null if it is not.
    Type
    DefaultConstantEvaluator.getCompileTimeConstantType(Expression expr)
    Returns the type of a constant expression if it is a compile-time constant as defined in the Java language specification, or null if it is not.
    Type
    DefaultSourceInfo.getType(Expression expr)
     
    Type
    KeYCrossReferenceSourceInfo.getType(Expression expr)
    clears the cache for the TypeReference to Type resolution.
    Type
    SourceInfo.getType(Expression expr)
    Returns the type of the given expression (may be null).
    boolean
    ConstantEvaluator.isCompileTimeConstant(Expression expr)
    Checks if the given expression is a compile-time constant as defined in the Java language specification.
    boolean
    ConstantEvaluator.isCompileTimeConstant(Expression expr, ConstantEvaluator.EvaluationResult res)
    Checks if the given expression is a compile-time constant as defined in the Java language specification, and derives the result.
    boolean
    DefaultConstantEvaluator.isCompileTimeConstant(Expression expr)
    Checks if the given expression is a compile-time constant as defined in the Java language specification.
    boolean
    DefaultConstantEvaluator.isCompileTimeConstant(Expression expr, ConstantEvaluator.EvaluationResult res)
    Checks if the given expression is a compile-time constant as defined in the Java language specification, and derives the result.
    boolean

    boolean
    SourceInfo.isNarrowingTo(Expression expr, PrimitiveType to)
    Checks automatic narrowing from compile-time constant ints to byte, char, or short.
